ab: ’ita’ rim’aen hila:an ta kakita’ kayzaeh ay?
ch: W:我們明天下午來碰面好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: kayzaeh, ’ita’ ’am rayno’ kakita’?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: ’ita’ ta kakita’ ’ay kakiSka:atan kayzaeh ay? yami ’am ’omalingo’.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: nak’isaza ray kararotonan ’am kayzaeh, yako ’am homiba: ’inoka SaySiyat a kayba:en.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: ray niya’om hito’ Singil So: minpongaeh kayzaeh a tomalan kita’en. So: minpongaeh ila So’o wa’ila ki:im ’iyakin!
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: nak’isaza ’am mayna:a’ tabin ray naba:an, honaehnge: a tomal!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: So: paSta’ay ila wa’ila!
ch: W: 那你巴斯達隘的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our paSta’ay!

ab: ’a:ay!
ch: M: 好啊!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: So’o ’ampowa’ lobih ray hito’ rowaSek?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: yako ’am lobih tomotoroe’ ka korkoring.
ch: M: 我要回去教小孩子。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: ’ampowa’ ’okik ray rahoeran tomotoroe’?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: yako maSriyae’ ’inmana’a hito’, ma’ ’iyakina matawaw no hito’ ma’an.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: So’o ra:am a tomal tomnon, hiya’ tomotoroe’ ’iSo’on?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: koko’ ma’an, siya ray niya’om hito’ ’ima wa’isan tomnon.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: So’o ’ampowa’ ’iyakina tatoroe’ tomnon?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: tomnon ka kayba:en koSa’en niya’om kaspengan, yako ’iyakina pataSilotor ka kaspengan.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: hini pinoSakan kayzaeh rae’oe:en.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: yako nonak pinaskayzaeh!
ch: W:是我自己釀的!
en: I made it myself!

ab: So’o wa’isan, hini ’am honaehnge: ay ’am poSak?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: ’oka’, ’aehae’ ka Sinangayan ma’ kayzaeh ila. ho’ol pilwaha:anen, ’isa:a’ pa’oSa’en ka tatamol ’ilo’en, ’isa:a’ kayzaeh ila ’aSkanen ray totowih ’izo’.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
